tagline: A Washington-area weekend guide that can pivot between events, food, and deals without turning into a cluttered dashboard.
summary: This file is generated by the starter research script. Right now it writes a small Washington-area queue that supports tabs and a calmer planning-oriented homepage.

items.title: Earth Day Fairfax returns to Lake Fairfax Park
items.description: Fairfax County's annual Earth Day celebration is set for Saturday, April 25, with outdoor activities, environmental booths, and family-friendly programming at Lake Fairfax Park.
items.reason: It is the easiest all-ages flagship pick in the region right now and gives the page a clear daytime anchor.

items.title: Arlington Festival of the Arts brings a bigger street-fair energy
items.description: Clarendon's festival weekend adds a larger-format art market feel, which makes it a strong pick for people who want more bustle than a single-venue event.
items.reason: It is a strong second pick for anyone who wants a more crowded, browse-heavy outing than a park event.

items.title: Art Blooms turns Mosaic District into an outdoor gallery
items.description: Mosaic District's Art Blooms installation runs through April with oversized floral displays, walkable installations, and plenty of reasons to linger around the shops and plaza.
items.reason: It gives the event lane a softer, lower-commitment option that still looks good on an image-led homepage.

items.title: FRESHFARM's Sunday market keeps the Mosaic routine easy
items.description: The Mosaic District farmers market continues on Sundays with produce, prepared foods, and a built-in coffee-and-stroll rhythm that works well for a low-key weekend plan.
items.reason: It is exactly the kind of dependable food find that makes the category useful before deeper restaurant monitoring exists.

items.title: Wine and Dine at Wolf Trap is a polished dinner pick in Vienna
items.description: Wolf Trap's Wine and Dine event at The Barns in Vienna offers a more polished night-out option for anyone open to a higher-effort dinner plan nearby.
items.reason: It gives the food lane one aspirational option instead of making the whole tab feel like markets and snack stops.

items.title: Fairfax's restaurant deals hub is a useful standing monitor
items.description: Visit Fairfax keeps a running page of restaurant specials, making it a strong seed source for a future local deals feed instead of only one-off sale finds.
items.reason: It is less flashy than a single coupon, but it is the more durable source if this becomes a recurring scrape.

items.title: Gringos & Mariachis lists weekday happy hour specials in Potomac
items.description: Visit Montgomery's directory notes recurring happy hour food and drink specials, which is the sort of dependable local offer worth tracking in a broader DMV deal lane.
items.reason: It starts to widen the page beyond Northern Virginia without losing the local utility angle.
